## psom {#psom}

Weekly snapshot of all PSOM Jira Service Management tickets.

One row per Jira issue per weekly snapshot. Each Monday run writes a full
snapshot of all current PSOM tickets to a new `date=` partition. Previous
partitions are preserved as an audit trail, enabling point-in-time analysis.

**Jira source:** `jtickets.atlassian.net`, project `PSOM`, fetched via
`POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` with full comment history via
`/rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment`.

`Keywords`: jira, psom, tickets, service requests, SLA, support

### Provenance

Queried from PSOM Jira Cloud via API by the `psom-weekly-sync` Lambda function.

* **Updated**: Weekly, every Monday at ~6:00 AM ET
* **Steward**: EDCP Data and Research Team
* **Contact**: [Yinlin Zhang](mailto:yinlin.zhang@cds-snc.ca) (Data and Research team) 

### Fields

One row per Jira issue per weekly snapshot (`date` partition).

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| date | varchar | Partition key — the date the Lambda ran (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Use in `WHERE date = '...'` filters. |
| key | varchar | Jira ticket key, e.g. `PSOM-42`. |
| summary | varchar | One-line ticket title. |
| description | varchar | Full ticket description, extracted from Atlassian Document Format (ADF) to plain text. |
| issue_type | varchar | Jira issue type name, e.g. `Email Request`, `Partner Support Request form`. |
| status | varchar | Current workflow status, e.g. `Done`, `Canceled`,  `Escalated to PTT`, `Escalated to Auth PT`, `Ready to archive` . |
| status_changed | varchar | ISO 8601 timestamp when the ticket's status category last changed. |
| priority | varchar | Priority level, e.g. `High`, `Normal`, `Low`, `Critical`. |
| assignee | varchar | Display name of the current assignee; empty string if unassigned. |
| reporter | varchar | Display name of the person who reported the ticket. |
| creator | varchar | Display name of the person who created the ticket (may differ from reporter). |
| created | varchar | ISO 8601 timestamp when the ticket was created. |
| updated | varchar | ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent field or comment update. |
| due_date | varchar | Due date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format; empty string if not set. |
| labels | varchar | Semicolon-separated list of labels applied to the ticket; empty string if none. |
| request_type | varchar | JSM service request type name, e.g. `New Access Request`. Empty for non-JSM tickets. |
| request_participants | varchar | Semicolon-separated display names of request participants (CC'd users). |
| organizations | varchar | Semicolon-separated names of JSM organizations the request is associated with, e.g. `Treasury Board Secretariat (TBS)`, `	Public Services and Procurement Canada (PSPC)`, `PrairiesCan`, `Veterans Affairs Canada`. |
| sla_first_response_due | varchar | ISO 8601 due timestamp for the "Time to first response" SLA; empty if SLA not active. |
| sla_first_response_breached | varchar | `"True"` if the first-response SLA was breached, `"False"` if met, `""` if not applicable. |
| sla_time_to_done_due | varchar | ISO 8601 due timestamp for the "Time to done" SLA; empty if SLA not active. |
| sla_time_to_done_breached | varchar | `"True"` if the time-to-done SLA was breached, `"False"` if met, `""` if not applicable. |
| comment_count | bigint | Total number of comments on the ticket. |
| comments | varchar | JSON array string of all comments. Each element: `{"author": "...", "created": "...", "updated": "...", "body": "...", "internal": false}`. The `body` is ADF-extracted plain text. The `internal` flag is `true` for JSM internal notes. |
| fetched_at | varchar | UTC timestamp when the Lambda wrote this row (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`). |

### Notes

All timestamp columns (`created`, `updated`, `status_changed`, `sla_*_due`,
`fetched_at`) are stored as `varchar`. Use `CAST(created AS TIMESTAMP)` in
Athena for date arithmetic.

SLA fields (`sla_first_response_*`, `sla_time_to_done_*`) are populated only
for tickets where a JSM SLA clock was active. Tickets created before the SLA
scheme was configured, or non-service-request issue types, will have empty SLA
columns. Exclude empty-string rows when calculating SLA breach rates.

Each weekly run adds a new `date=` partition. All previous partitions are
retained, so you can compare snapshots across weeks to track status changes over
time.
